name: Gorontalo

sources.author: Steinhauer, Hein
sources.title: Sentence-initial elements in Gorontalo
sources.notes: Descriptive source on Gorontalo; a starting point for a future grapheme map.

sources.author: Ager, Simon
sources.title: Gorontalo language, alphabet and pronunciation
sources.notes: Letter→IPA chart for the Gorontalo Latin alphabet: b [b/ɓ], c [c], d [d/ɗ/d̠], j [ɟ], q [ʔ], ng [ŋ], ny [ɲ], y [j], vowels a e i o u.

sources.author: Little, Elizabeth
sources.title: Gorontalo phonology (in: Sneddon, J. N. (ed.), Studies in Sulawesi Linguistics IV)
sources.notes: Five-vowel system and penultimate stress; consonant inventory including the implosives /ɓ ɗ/ and the laminal /d̠/.

notes: Gorontalo (Bahasa Hulontalo) — Philippine-type Austronesian language of northern Sulawesi, Indonesia, written today in an Indonesian-based Latin alphabet (historically also in Jawi/Pegon Arabic script, which is out of scope). The map commits to the modern Latin orthography as tabulated by Omniglot, cross-checked against the phoneme inventory in the Wikipedia phonology (Little 1995; Steinhauer). Five vowels ⟨a e i o u⟩ = /a e i o u/. Consonants: ⟨ng⟩ = /ŋ/, ⟨ny⟩ = /ɲ/, ⟨c⟩ = the voiceless PALATAL stop /c/ (not the Indonesian affricate /tʃ/), ⟨j⟩ = /ɟ/, ⟨q⟩ = the glottal stop /ʔ/ (also written with an apostrophe in some texts, e.g. wala'o 'child'), ⟨y⟩ = /j/. The letters ⟨b⟩ and ⟨d⟩ are ambiguous: each spells both the plain voiced stop and the corresponding IMPLOSIVE (/b ɓ/, /d ɗ/), and ⟨d⟩ additionally spells the laminal post-alveolar /d̠/ (written ⟨ḓ⟩ only in linguistic materials) — that contrast is NOT recoverable from ordinary spelling, so the candidates are listed plain-first. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script Gorontalo in modern Indonesian-based spelling.

ancestors.notes: Austronesian — the classification node this language sits under.
